articles.title: Curiosity Rover Sniffs Out DNA-Like Molecule on Mars!
articles.summary: NASA’s Curiosity rover found a molecule on Mars that looks like a piece of DNA—something never seen before on the Red Planet. The rover also found other organic chemicals in 3.5-billion-year-old clay. Scientists can’t yet tell if these came from ancient life or from space rocks.

articles.title: Antimatter Acts Like a Wave—Scientists Just Proved It!
articles.summary: For the first time, scientists have seen antimatter behave like a wave. They shot a beam of positronium—a tiny “atom” made of an electron and its antimatter twin—through a sheet of graphene and got a wavy interference pattern. This proves that even exotic particles follow the weird rules of quantum physics.

articles.title: How a Computer Program Called Lean Is Changing Math Forever
articles.summary: A new book tells the story of Lean, a computer program that can check math proofs for mistakes. Once a tool for software, Lean is now used by thousands of mathematicians and AI researchers. It even helped an AI score a silver medal at the 2024 Math Olympiad!
